mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below one percent compresses the risk-free discount rate and stimulates credit-sensitive corporate investment, which mechanically raises the present value of future earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 1%, the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed months, or the mean return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 5.5% signals that the economy has reached full employment, which triggers expectations of imminent Federal Reserve tightening and tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate a demand-supply expansion where revenue growth outpaces cost pressures, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint event of rising RSAFS and rising INDPRO fails to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term carries a negative sign.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 signals sustainable demand without provoking tightening fears, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in industrial production signals a negative profit impulse as operating leverage turns unfavorable and inventory liquidation begins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: A three-month decline in INDPRO coincides with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observed episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in credit-sensitive construction activity and durable goods demand, which reduces aggregate income expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,200, the S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and a declining unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases and UNRATE decreases fails to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, with positive returns occurring in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in monthly retail sales growth directly lifts near-term revenue forecasts for consumer-facing corporations, causing an immediate upward repricing of the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation is below 0.02.
